name: Azul C4 — The Pauseless Lineage
summary: Azul's Continuously Concurrent Compacting Collector (production since 2010) is the direct ancestor of ZGC and Shenandoah. Generational pauseless collector using a Loaded Value Barrier read barrier to support concurrent compaction, concurrent remapping, and concurrent incremental-update tracing.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Azul's **Continuously Concurrent Compacting Collector** (C4, production since 2010) is the direct ancestor of ZGC and Shenandoah. A generational **pauseless** collector using a Loaded Value Barrier (LVB) read barrier to support concurrent compaction, concurrent remapping, and concurrent incremental-update tracing.

The sole collector in Azul Prime (formerly Zing).

sections.title: Loaded Value Barrier and self-healing
sections.content:
**LVB read barrier** ensures every loaded reference is "self-healed" to a safe form, allowing relocation without STW remap. The barrier checks each loaded reference against the colored-pointer state and, if needed, redirects through the forwarding word and writes the corrected pointer back into the source field.

This is the technique ZGC adopted (colored pointers + load barrier + self-healing) and that Shenandoah evolved from Brooks-style forwarding pointers into. Both inherit C4's invariant that a barrier slow path must run at most once per location.

sections.title: Simultaneous-generational concurrency
sections.content:
**Simultaneous-generational concurrency**: young and old generation collections run concurrently and independently, never blocking each other. This is stronger than Generational ZGC's design: in C4, neither generation's collector can be paused or starved by the other.

OS-kernel virtual-memory enhancements (originally on Linux) sustain the high VM mapping rate required for pauseless operation. The kernel changes — fast `madvise`/`munmap` paths, optimised TLB shootdowns — were upstreamed in part and are visible in modern Linux kernels.

sections.title: Deployment and significance
sections.content:
C4 is the sole collector in Azul Prime (formerly Zing). Status (as of 2026-04): commercial JVM distribution; the architectural ancestor of OpenJDK ZGC and Shenandoah.

The lesson: **pauseless concurrent compaction was production-ready a decade before OpenJDK had it**. Azul's contribution to the JVM ecosystem is largely the demonstration that the design point exists and the gradual upstreaming of techniques into OpenJDK via the ZGC and Shenandoah projects.

sources.label: C4 — The Continuously Concurrent Compacting Collector (ISMM 2011)
